A.rationale: Correct O(n) seen-set fix, tests pass; but wrongly claims original raised TypeError on unhashable via 'in result' (list uses ==, handles them).

B.rationale: Correct linear seen-set rewrite, order preserved, tests pass; accurate hashability note, tightest answer with no false claim.

C.rationale: Correct fix, tests pass, most precise on original '==' handling; fewest turns/fastest. Slightly verbose persona-style question costs conciseness.

D.rationale: Correct O(n) fix, tests pass; hashability note hedged imprecisely ('can't compare with == reliably'), most turns (6) for same result.

notes: All four: same seen-set O(n) fix, order preserved, 3/3 pass. B tightest and accurate. C technically sharpest but wordier; A's claim original raised TypeError is wrong; D imprecise and slowest.
